scenario: dotenv adapter validate(component, changes, ctx) → Diagnostic[] — the dangerous-key scan. One clean env (→[]), one env with dangerous keys (PATH + NODE_OPTIONS), and the secret files (bare secret NOT scanned; secret .env scanned but clean). Mirrors lint/all-types.json's perType shape. SPECIFICATION golden.
adapterStatus: NOT IMPLEMENTED as a dotenv adapter — but the dangerous-key scan IS coded (src/commands/lint/env-key-rules.ts isDangerousVaultKey + src/core/adapter/adapters/akm-lint.ts dangerousEnvKeyDiagnostics). The `issue` code and scan narrowness below are code-grounded.
specSource: docs/architecture/specs/akm-0.9.0-bundle-adapter-spec.md §6 (env validation column: 'dangerous-key warn scan'; secret: 'dangerous-key scan; classifyBySmartMd bails on secrets/'), §2/§5.1 (Diagnostic {file, issue, detail, fixed}).
notes: SPECIFICATION golden. SCAN NARROWNESS (code-grounded, preserved by the adapter port): the dangerous-key scan runs ONLY on *.env-suffixed files. So env/dangerous.env is scanned (ref prefix env:), secrets/ci.env is scanned (ref prefix secret:, because it ends .env), but secrets/deploy-key (BARE filename, no .env suffix) is NOT scanned at all — its whole content is an opaque secret value that is never read.

`dangerous-vault-key` is the exact code the akm-lint port emits; the `detail` here mirrors src/commands/lint/env-key-rules.ts checkVaultForDangerousKeys (suppressible with a `# akm-lint-ok: dangerous-vault-key` comment on the preceding line).
The scan reads KEY NAMES only; env values are never read even to check them (only the key identifier matters for the hijack vector).

envClean.note: FIXTURE_GREETING, LOG_LEVEL are not in DANGEROUS_VAULT_KEYS → no findings.

issues.detail: Env key `PATH` can be used to hijack process execution when injected via `akm env run`. Review this file before running `akm env run` against untrusted stashes. (suppress with: # akm-lint-ok: dangerous-vault-key on the previous line)

Env key `NODE_OPTIONS` can be used to hijack process execution when injected via `akm env run`. Review this file before running `akm env run` against untrusted stashes. (suppress with: # akm-lint-ok: dangerous-vault-key on the previous line)

envDangerous.note: PATH and NODE_OPTIONS are in DANGEROUS_VAULT_KEYS; APP_TOKEN is not. Two findings, one per dangerous key, in file order.

secretBareNotScanned.note: BARE filename (no .env suffix) → NOT scanned. The whole file is the secret value and is never read (dangerousEnvKeyDiagnostics returns [] when !endsWith('.env')).

secretEnvScannedClean.note: ends .env → scanned (ref prefix secret:), but CI_DEPLOY_TOKEN / REGISTRY_USER are not dangerous → []. NOTE: only the dangerous-key SCAN reads key names here; the RENDERER still redacts this to name-only (type=secret), see renderer.json.
